content:
# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - Overview

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- Complex projects requiring multiple review perspectives
- Projects with UI (design review needed)
- Projects with API (DX review needed)
- Security-sensitive projects (security review needed)
- Team collaboration across multiple agents

Inputs required:
- Project type (web/backend/automation/desktop)
- Risk profile (authentication? payments? user data?)
- Available AI models for cross-model review

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.

# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - Execution Steps

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Identify required quality gates for the project type
- Define each gate's checklist and stop condition
- Set the gate execution order (sequential, not parallel)
- Execute gates at the correct stage (Plan/Review/Test/Ship)
- Record gate results for retro

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.

# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - API Calls

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.

- POST /brief to retrieve domain rules for gate criteria

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.

# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations
- Each role has a defined boundary and checklist
- Cross-model agreement is signal, not proof — user has final say
- Not all 23 roles apply to every project — select based on project needs

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.

# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - Verification

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Each required gate has been executed
- Gate results documented with pass/fail and evidence
- Failed gates have been resolved or escalated

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.

# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - Failure Modes

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Applying all 23 roles to a simple script (overhead)
- Using role names without defining actual checklists
- Assuming two-model agreement equals correctness
- Skipping required gates for speed

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.

# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - Handoff Report

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- List which gates were used and results
- Note any new constraints discovered

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.
